theme: an engineer buying cables one truck at a time
title: Cable Trucks to the Substations
strategies: myopic-greedy baseline: on each truck (the program is invoked once per truck, online), buy it iff it is affordable right now AND strictly raises the exact lambda_2 of the network built so far -- reproduces the evaluator's internal weak reference and burns the budget on many small-but-real early gains
reject-everything trivial: keep only the free backbone, spend nothing

spectral-leverage adaptive threshold pacing (strong): each call, take a cheap Fiedler-vector sketch of the network built so far, score this truck's cable by (x_u - x_v)^2 / cost, spend an initial slice of trucks purely observing that distribution (via the private cross-call 'state' blob), then accept only cables clearing a percentile bar that adapts to (budget remaining / trucks remaining) -- correctly recognizes that decoy cables inside an already-adequate cluster barely move the Fiedler vector while cables crossing the bottleneck move it a lot, and paces spending so budget survives to the late high-leverage cables it can never see in advance
greedy plus arbitrary fixed absolute threshold on marginal gain: same myopic failure mode as the reference baseline, just with a different cutoff
